findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 44. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Fabricated email address in the HTML. The PDF's digital signature block shows 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc' followed by 'denr.gov' (split across lines), but the HTML constructs a clickable mailto link 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov'. While the intent may be similar, the PDF actually shows 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov' split as 'elizabeth.werner@nc' and 'denr.gov', and the HTML creates a hyperlink that does not exist in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'email=elizabeth.werner@nc denr.gov' (line-broken in signature metadata). HTML: 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov' — a clickable mailto link not present in the PDF.

findings.description: Fabricated hyperlink. The HTML creates a mailto: link for the email address in the digital signature block. The source PDF contains no hyperlinks — the email address appears only as metadata text in the digital signature stamp.
findings.evidence: HTML: 'elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov'. PDF: no hyperlinks present; email appears as plain text in digital signature metadata.

findings.description: Fabricated internal navigation links in the table of contents. The HTML adds anchor href links (#history-background, etc.) to the outline items, creating navigation that does not exist in the source PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML TOC items: 'History/Background', etc. PDF: plain numbered outline text with no hyperlinks.

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is set as h1, but the actual section headings (I. History/Background, II. Public Meeting, III. Written Comments, IV. Attachments) are set as h2. This is acceptable. However, there is a nested <main> element inside the outer <main>, which is a structural HTML error though not a heading hierarchy issue per se.

The full content of page 1 appears to be duplicated. The header information (State of NC, addresses, officials, MEMORANDUM, date, To/From/Subject, outline) appears once in the <header> section and then again in the <main> section, effectively rendering the first page content twice.
findings.evidence: Header block contains: 'State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management', PAT MCCRORY/Governor, etc. Then the <main> section repeats MEMORANDUM, April 28, 2016, To/From/Subject blocks and the entire outline again.

findings.description: An embedded image placeholder appears between the table of contents and Section I that does not correspond to meaningful content separation in the PDF. This appears to be a page-break artifact.
findings.evidence: HTML: '' inserted between the TOC and Section I.

findings.description: The second image has empty alt text, providing no accessibility value. It appears to be a decorative or page-break artifact.
findings.evidence: HTML: '' — empty alt attribute.
